name: Hokkaido Baked Cheese Tarts
instructions:
1. Start by preparing the shortcrust pastry. Place the butter in the freezer for 10-15 minutes until very cold but not frozen solid. Meanwhile, sift together the flour, icing sugar, almond flour, and a pinch of salt into a large bowl to ensure no lumps and even distribution.

2. Using your fingertips, quickly rub the chilled butter into the dry mixture, working it between your fingers until it resembles coarse sand with no large butter chunks remaining. This creates the flaky texture. Make a well in the center, add the egg, and gently knead just until the dough comes together into a smooth ball—don't overwork or it will become tough. Roll out the dough between two silicone mats to an even 2mm thickness, rotating occasionally for uniformity.

3. Transfer the rolled dough (still between mats) to the freezer for 10-15 minutes to firm up, making it easier to cut cleanly. Remove and cut circles slightly larger than your tart molds using a cookie cutter or glass. Gently press the dough circles into the molds, ensuring they fit snugly against the sides and bottom with no air pockets. Freeze for 1 hour to prevent shrinkage during baking.

4. Preheat your oven to 170°C (340°F). Pre-bake the frozen tart shells directly from the freezer for 10 minutes until they're lightly golden and set but not browned. Remove from oven and allow them to cool completely on a wire rack.

5. Prepare the creamy filling by beating together the cream cheese, grated Parmesan, butter, and sugar in a bowl until completely smooth and fluffy with no lumps. Add the egg yolks one at a time, mixing well after each addition. Sift in the corn starch to prevent clumping, then gradually pour in the milk and heavy cream, blending until the mixture is silky and well combined.

6. Carefully pour the filling into the cooled tart shells, filling them about 80% full to allow for slight expansion. Bake at 170°C (340°F) for 12-15 minutes, watching carefully. The filling is done when the edges are set but the center still has a gentle wobble when you shake the pan—it will firm up as it cools.

7. Remove from oven and let the tarts cool in their molds for 10 minutes, then carefully unmold. These tarts are delicious served warm with their creamy, custardy center, or chilled for a firmer, cheesecake-like texture.
simpleInstructions:
1. Chill butter, then mix with dry ingredients until sandy.

2. Add egg, roll dough thin, cut circles, and freeze in molds.

3. Pre-bake shells until lightly golden, then cool.

4. Blend cream cheese filling until smooth and pour into shells.

5. Bake until just set with a wobble. Serve warm or chilled.
